page: Amazon Web Services m7i.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs derived from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz and is equipped with a 105 MB L3 cache. The instance includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark results position the m7i.4xlarge primarily in the average performance tier for most compute and memory tasks, with notable strengths in specific workloads. Single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench text processing perform in the top 25% tier, while text generation for small language models reaches the top 10% tier at 333.79 tokens per second. Other operations, including Redis SET operations at 20.92 million operations per second, static web serving throughput at 27.75 GB/sec, and Passmark database operations, fall within the average range. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.4xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, which is ideal for workloads relying on network-attached storage. This instance is well-suited for applications such as web serving, software compilation, database operations, and light machine learning inference tasks that leverage its AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets.
description: The Amazon Web Services m7i.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores. Operating at 3.2 GHz, it features 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place the m7i.4xlarge in the average tier for most tasks, though it exhibits strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and top-tier text generation for small language models. Supported by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the processor includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 memory, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for balanced compute and memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix multiplication extensions for versatile workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor
64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Advanced instruction support including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Strong performance in text processing and small-model text generation
Best suited for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
